title: Idea: opennig http/https in external OS browser
author: jackmoscow
body_snippet:
Is it possible to implement the possibility of using an external OS browser? (HTTP/HTTPS protocols) 
Default or which could be selected manually

## Expected Behavior
Make it possible to select the OS browser (default or another installed in the OS)

## Current Behavior
At the moment http/https opens in the built-in program, the connection does not always occur, especially if self-signed certificates are used

## Possible Solution
When setting up an external browser, a redirect to a li...

iterations.description: Implement the requested feature to open HTTP/HTTPS links in an external OS browser for better user experience, centralizing URL opening logic.

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet:
Which Version of mRemoteNG are you using? 

Starting with 1.77 (please try out the nightly build) it should use Chromium as base which uses the Builtin Windows Certificate Store, previously it was something Firefox-Based with the option to use IE which caused some trouble (see #1842).

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet: Just noted in #1842 that this feature seems to not work as it should right now also with Version 1.77.2-nb (20220107). IE rendering Engine is unable to handle the new crypto standards, and the "Edge Chromium" engine just does display nothing which is quite unfortunate...

notes:
AI triage (gemini): Implement the requested feature to open HTTP/HTTPS links in an external OS browser for better user experience, centralizing URL opening logic.
Approach: Create a new utility class `UrlTools` to centralize the logic for opening URLs in an external browser. Modify existing HTTP/HTTPS protocol handling and context menus to utilize this new utility for launching URLs externally.
